title: Week 07 - Arrays and Table Data
description: Practice array reading, modification, swapping, traversal, copying, and state recording.

questions.title: Read the Score of a Student at a Given Position

description.background: The teacher stores student scores in an array scores. Each position corresponds to one student's score.
description.task: Return the score of the student at position index.
description.input: One array scores and one number index.
description.output: Return the value at position index in scores.
description.hint: Each position in an array can be accessed by its index.

questions.title: Modify the Points for One Day in a Daily Points Table

description.background: The points a player earns each day are stored in the array points. One day's point record is wrong and needs to be changed to newValue.
description.task: Change the points for day day to newValue, then return the updated points for that day.
description.input: One array points and two numbers: day and newValue.
description.output: Return the updated points for day day.
description.hint: After finding the matching position, you can directly update the data there.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(points, day, newValue)
    points[day] = newValue
    return points[day]
end

questions.title: Swap the Data at Two Positions in a Student Line

description.background: Student numbers in a line are stored in the array students. The teacher wants to swap the students at positions a and b.
description.task: Swap the data at two positions in the array and return the swapped array.
description.input: One array students and two numbers: a and b.
description.output: Return the students array after the swap.
description.hint: When swapping two positions, you can use a temporary variable.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(students, a, b)
    local temp = students[a]
    students[a] = students[b]
    students[b] = temp
    return students
end

questions.title: Traverse a Score Sheet and Count Passing Students

description.background: The teacher stores the scores of n students in the array scores. A score of 60 or above is passing.
description.task: Traverse the score sheet and count how many students pass.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the number of passing students.
description.hint: When traversing the array, check the score at one position each time.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local count = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 60 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Copy a Class Score Sheet as a Backup

description.background: The teacher wants to copy the score sheet scores so the original data will not be lost if the original score sheet is modified later.
description.task: Generate a new array backup that stores the same data as scores.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.hint: To copy an array, put each position's data into the new array one by one.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local backup = {}

    for i = 1, n do
        backup[i] = scores[i]
    end

    return backup
end

questions.title: Add an Event Bonus to Every Item in a Daily Coin Table

description.background: A game holds an event, and each day's coin reward increases by bonus. The original coin records are stored in the array coins.
description.task: Increase every day's coin value in coins by bonus and return the updated array.
description.input: One array coins, one number n, and one number bonus.
description.hint: Apply the same update to every position in the array.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, n, bonus)
    for i = 1, n do
        coins[i] = coins[i] + bonus
    end

    return coins
end

questions.title: Generate a Daily Accumulated Points Table for Upgrade Progress

description.background: The points a player earns each day are stored in the array points. To view upgrade progress, a new accumulated points table needs to be generated.
description.task: Generate the array totals, where totals[i] is the sum of points from day 1 through day i.
description.input: One array points and one number n.
description.output: Return the accumulated points array totals.
description.hint: Traverse the daily points while recording the total points so far.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(points, n)
    local totals = {}
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        sum = sum + points[i]
        totals[i] = sum
    end

    return totals
end

questions.title: Generate the New Line Order After a Team Turns Around

description.background: Student numbers in a line are stored in the array students. After the teacher says to turn around, the original last student becomes first, and the original first student becomes last.
description.task: Generate the new array after the team turns around.
description.input: One array students and one number n.
description.hint: The front positions of the new array can come from the back positions of the original array.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(students, n)
    local result = {}

    for i = 1, n do
        result[i] = students[n - i + 1]
    end

    return result
end

questions.title: Record Who Has the Ball After Each Round in a Passing Game

description.background: n students stand in a circle and play a passing game. The ball starts with student start. Each round, the ball is passed forward by steps positions, and we need to record who has the ball after each round.
description.task: Simulate rounds rounds of passing and return an array recording the student number holding the ball after each round.
description.input: Four numbers: n, start, steps, and rounds.
description.output: Return an array recording which student has the ball after each round.
description.hint: When the position passes the last student, it needs to wrap back to the start of the line.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n, start, steps, rounds)
    local record = {}
    local position = start

    for i = 1, rounds do
        position = position + steps
        while position > n do
            position = position - n
        end
        record[i] = position
    end

    return record
end
